skylight_Vessel.Name: NA
Sister.Ship.Non.Compliance.Description: ADMIRAL KOLCHAK (9076208) was documented fishing 0.5nm inside the US EEZ by a USCG helicopter in 2014. The US issued the vessel owner a Notice of Violation and Assessment of civil penalty and sent the notice to the Russian Ministry of Justice for service of process on the vessel owner, but it appears there was no enforcement by Russia.  IKHTIOLOG (8804309) source indicates that a total of nine incursions over a period of four days were detected into the US EEZ while the vessel was fishing close to the Russia-US boundary in May/June 2022 (from wording it sounds like this was detected remotely, presumably over AIS). The Russian Border Guard investigated based on USCG report and fined the vessel owner for  failing to notify the Russian Federation of an MBL crossing. Source

skylight_Vessel.Name: NA
Sister.Ship.Non.Compliance.Description: FU YUAN YU 019 IATTC-authorized fishing vessel— retrieving longline gear in the IATTC Convention Area about 400 miles southwest of the Mexican coast. Sea Shepherd witnessed the vessel using metal branch lines running directly from longline floats in violation of a CMM (C-16-05) that prohibits the use of this type of gear, known as shark lines.
